Notes: Keeps existing movies or episodes when their Sonarr/Radarr source is temporarily unavailable.
Explicitly protects plugin HTTP endpoints and validates connection-test URLs.
Adds a concise Readme dialog with storage, workflow and supported-version information.

notes: Status badge now reflects the release type: IN CINEMAS, ON DISC (physical) or DIGITAL.
New setting to place the status badge in any of the four corners (the date stays bottom-right; the badge sits above it when they share that corner).
The info window now shows only the newest release note.
Bigger, more readable poster/backdrop badges (existing entries are re-rendered automatically on the next refresh).
"Days ahead" can now go up to 365 days (was capped at 120).
Fixed the list of rows growing/duplicating on every plugin update or restart (config default no longer appended by the serializer). Existing duplicates are cleaned up automatically.
Built against Emby 4.9.5.0 so the plugin also loads on the current stable, not only on 4.10 beta (compile against the lowest supported version; it rolls forward to newer ones).
Trailers are now local trailer extras, so Emby shows its native "Trailer" button; the main entry stays a short placeholder.
Removed the placeholder-marker machinery and some unused fields.
The overview is now fetched in the plugin's language too, not just the badges (via the library's preferred metadata language).
Series trailers are fetched once per series and reused for every episode instead of searching per episode.
A shared trailer is now downloaded once and copied, not re-downloaded per folder.
Language switch now takes effect on the next refresh - no need to delete and recreate the libraries.
Every settings field now has a short description explaining what it's for.
Info screen shows the TMDB key help first, then the changelog.
Trailers now download as a ready-to-play stream (up to 720p) needing no ffmpeg - fixes trailers never appearing on images without a usable ffmpeg.
Leftover download fragments are cleaned up when a merge fails.
ffmpeg is now optional and only used if you set an explicit path to a modern build.
Attempted ffmpeg path resolution via Emby's startup argument (superseded by 0.5.3.0).
Fixed yt-dlp auto-download picking the Python-dependent build instead of the correct standalone binary.
Fixed a GLIBC conflict where Emby's own bundled libc broke yt-dlp (and other subprocesses) entirely on some images.
Poster/backdrop badges are now re-rendered on every refresh, not just once - date/language/list changes show up immediately.
Fixed placeholder trailers never being replaced by a real one once yt-dlp becomes available.
"In Cinemas" badge also added to the backdrop/hero image.
Blocked Emby's own online image fetchers for Coming Soon libraries so a later scan can't silently replace our badged images.
Bigger, more readable log viewer, plus an "open in new tab" option.
Release notes now list every change per version instead of one squashed line.
API key fields explain where to find the key.
Portable yt-dlp/ffmpeg resolution: uses Emby's own ffmpeg automatically, auto-downloads yt-dlp if missing - works on any Docker image.
Log viewer on the settings page.
Backdrop images for the home-screen hero.
"In Cinemas" poster badge.
Full German translation of the settings page.
Daily 00:15 default schedule instead of hourly.
Multiple lists, each its own home-screen row.
Smaller badge text.
English/German language for badges, dates and descriptions.
Restructured settings page (collapsed sections, Info button).
Cast/crew now fetched by Emby's own metadata provider.
Fixed a command-injection risk in trailer downloads.
Writes real poster/video/NFO files so Emby's own scanner indexes them.
Redesigned poster badges (rounded pills, centered text, green UPCOMING badge).
Coming Soon library auto-created and kept in sync every refresh.
